title: ReplayWindow accepts frame at counter 0 on wrap-around edge case
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/replay.py:97-106

After re-analysis: This is NOT a bug. The half-space arithmetic is correct by design.

The boundary at diff=0x800000 treats frames exactly 8,388,608 positions 'ahead' as old/replay. This is intentional: frames more than half the counter space away could be either very old OR very far ahead, so the conservative choice is to reject.

The WRAPAROUND_WARNING_THRESHOLD at 0xFF0000 ensures re-keying happens before we get near this ambiguous zone. This is defense in depth.

Downgrading to P4 (documentation). The threshold behavior should be documented for implementers.
